chart.title: Share covered by at least one social protection benefit
chart.subtitle: The percentage of the population that either benefits from or contributes to at least one social protection scheme. This includes programs such as maternity benefits, unemployment benefits, and pensions.

1.3.1 - [ILO] Proportion of population c.titleShort: 1.3.1 - [ILO] Proportion of population covered by at least one social protection benefit, by sex (%) - SI_COV_BENFTS - Both sexes
1.3.1 - [ILO] Proportion of population c.titleLong: 1.3.1 - [ILO] Proportion of population covered by at least one social protection benefit, by sex (%) - SI_COV_BENFTS - Both sexes
1.3.1 - [ILO] Proportion of population c.descriptionShort: [ILO] Proportion of population covered by at least one social protection benefit, by sex (%)
descriptionKey: This indicator is calculated as the ratio of the population receiving cash benefits under at least one of multiple contingencies/social protection functions or actively contributing to at least one social security scheme to the total population. The different benefits are aimed at children, unemployed persons, older persons, persons with disabilities, pregnant women, newborns, work-injury victims and the poor and the vulnerable.
1.3.1 - [ILO] Proportion of population c.citationShort: International Labour Organization – with minor processing by Our World in Data
